content.introduction: Sorting algorithm analysis compares methods by complexity, stability, memory, and workload fit to guide practical selection.

sections.title: 7.11.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define key evaluation dimensions: time complexity, space complexity, stability, adaptivity, and in-place property.
examples: Compare stable vs unstable sorts for record processing
Explain in-place constraint in memory-limited environments

sections.title: 7.11.2 Representation and Workflow
examples: Build a decision table for common sorting methods
Evaluate nearly sorted vs random input scenarios

sections.title: 7.11.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Provide engineering decision guidelines based on data size, distribution, memory limits, and latency requirements.
examples: Choose merge sort for stable large-data tasks
Choose quick sort for fast average in-memory sorting
Choose heap sort when worst-case bounds and low memory are required

keyTakeaways: Analyze sorting methods with multiple criteria
Match algorithm choice to input and constraints
Balance average speed against worst-case guarantees
Account for stability and memory requirements
Make evidence-based sorting decisions in engineering practice
